essence: Oversees CNC production operations, manages teams, optimises workflow, and ensures high‑quality, efficient manufacturing output.

identity.title: CNC Production Manager
identity.summary: Leads CNC machining operations, manages production schedules, ensures quality and efficiency, and drives continuous improvement across manufacturing environments.

salary.notes: Managers in aerospace, defence, or multi‑facility operations can exceed £95k-£120k+.

qualifications: Extensive experience in CNC machining, programming, or team leadership
Knowledge of production planning, workflow optimisation, and lean manufacturing
Ability to manage teams, budgets, and performance metrics
Understanding of quality control, safety standards, and industrial processes

lifestyle.description: CNC production managers work in precision engineering firms, aerospace, automotive, and industrial manufacturing. They oversee production lines, manage teams, optimise processes, and ensure output meets quality and efficiency targets. The role suits individuals who enjoy leadership, operational strategy, and improving manufacturing performance.

habits: Manages CNC production teams and workflows
Oversees scheduling, capacity planning, and resource allocation
Optimises machining processes and reduces downtime
Drives continuous improvement and operational efficiency
